:row.:haystack: fuzz round 4: packs, cache corruption, hostile directory names adversarial coverage for the newest surfaces: garbage or lying pack indexes and truncated packs must fail well; a corrupt derived cache is a miss, never a crash or a lie the fingerprint would miss; and directory names with spaces or edn-hostile characters must not corrupt events on write when rollout or context facts keywordize them. :feature round 4 caught corrupt-on-write; mint now refuses the unreadable dfc76f9 :green
:row.:title: fuzz round 4: packs, cache corruption, hostile directory names
:row.:describe: Adversarial coverage for the newest surfaces: garbage or lying pack indexes and truncated packs must fail well; a corrupt derived cache is a miss, never a crash or a lie the fingerprint would miss; and directory names with spaces or EDN-hostile characters must not corrupt events on write when rollout or context facts keywordize them.

:row.:haystack: h11 (authoring ladder): a non-developer authors a process given the authoring lens, a domain expert produces a linted, simulated, test-passing process for their own workflow without editing edn every production process was ultimately hand-written by a developer — then the authoring layer is decoration and the adoption ceiling stands tik author (commit 31ffd53)
:row.:title: H11 (authoring ladder): a non-developer authors a process
:row.:describe: Given the authoring lens, a domain expert produces a linted, simulated, test-passing process for their own workflow without editing EDN

:row.:haystack: unbreakable surface: top-level landing, per-sink isolation, garbage-config fuzzing the cli lands every escape: a final handler turns ex-info into its one-line message and anything else into a this-is-a-bug line, both exit 1, traces only under tik_debug=1. config readers name their file. effects run isolates sink failures — one dead endpoint no longer abandons the rest. fuzz round 3 feeds malformed edn to every config-consuming command and demands words, never traces. :feature every escape lands as words; one dead sink cannot sink the rest dd104e9 :green
:row.:title: unbreakable surface: top-level landing, per-sink isolation, garbage-config fuzzing
:row.:describe: The CLI lands every escape: a final handler turns ex-info into its one-line message and anything else into a this-is-a-bug line, both exit 1, traces only under TIK_DEBUG=1. Config readers name their file. effects run isolates sink failures — one dead endpoint no longer abandons the rest. Fuzz round 3 feeds malformed EDN to every config-consuming command and demands words, never traces.

:row.:haystack: harden cross-runtime test against babashka stdout noise (marker protocol) :bug make the cross-runtime differential immune to incidental babashka stdout via a u+0001 line marker, fixing a rare full-suite line-count flake, and report the diverging value on failure the cross-runtime test now measures agreement robustly, not babashka stdout noise 72f700e :green
:row.:title: harden cross-runtime test against babashka stdout noise (marker protocol)
:row.:describe: make the cross-runtime differential immune to incidental babashka stdout via a U+0001 line marker, fixing a rare full-suite line-count flake, and report the diverging value on failure

:row.:haystack: tik author check: lint answers.edn before it becomes a process a malli schema plus smell heuristics over the authoring answers format: flag-shaped fact names, activity-named stages, needless stages that derive instantly, placeholder role members. author --from validates before writing; author check <file> reports without writing. the llm prompt teaches the same rules so models stop producing the smells. :feature answers files lint before they build; the llm prompt teaches the same rules 7bac1f6 :green
:row.:title: tik author check: lint answers.edn before it becomes a process
:row.:describe: A malli schema plus smell heuristics over the authoring answers format: flag-shaped fact names, activity-named stages, needless stages that derive instantly, placeholder role members. author --from validates before writing; author check <file> reports without writing. The LLM prompt teaches the same rules so models stop producing the smells.

:row.:haystack: packfiles: settled tickets consolidate into content-addressed packs tik pack concatenates a settled ticket's exact per-event hashed byte regions into one events.pack plus an offset index, sidecars carried along; readers merge pack and loose files so appends keep working; verify slices the pack and checks each region against its id; the derived cache fingerprints a packed ticket by the pack's own content address. inode pressure drops from dozens of files per settled ticket to two. :feature one pack per settled ticket; the slices still hash to their ids 68c2e3c :green
:row.:title: packfiles: settled tickets consolidate into content-addressed packs
:row.:describe: tik pack concatenates a settled ticket's exact per-event hashed byte regions into one events.pack plus an offset index, sidecars carried along; readers merge pack and loose files so appends keep working; verify slices the pack and checks each region against its id; the derived cache fingerprints a packed ticket by the pack's own content address. Inode pressure drops from dozens of files per settled ticket to two.

:row.:haystack: tik rollout: one ticket per repo plus a parent whose links are the checklist tik rollout <process> scans the store holder for git repos, creates one context-tagged ticket per uncovered repo, and wires them as link facts on a parent (reused via --parent or by title, else created) — a checklist whose checkmarks derive themselves. re-runs are idempotent and report coverage. :feature rollout scans repos, wires the living checklist, re-runs report coverage 78b70e1 :green
:row.:title: tik rollout: one ticket per repo plus a parent whose links are the checklist
:row.:describe: tik rollout <process> scans the store holder for git repos, creates one context-tagged ticket per uncovered repo, and wires them as link facts on a parent (reused via --parent or by title, else created) — a checklist whose checkmarks derive themselves. Re-runs are idempotent and report coverage.

:row.:haystack: alert effects: derived transitions out to slack/discord/matrix/alertmanager/pagerduty tik effects run: newly-derived stage transitions fire webhooks per effects.edn adapters; idempotency via content-hashed effect keys in a disposable ledger (adr 0013 — delete it and worst case is a resend); delivery never touches ticket truth (adr 0019) :feature 4aac5cf :green

:row.:haystack: env interpolation for sink headers: secrets stay in the environment sink header values resolve at send time: literal strings, {:env name} from the process environment, or {:command [pass show x]} from any local secret manager. effects.edn commits cleanly with zero secrets; failures name the header and source. :feature secrets stay in env or the password manager, never in tik 7de750d :green
:row.:title: env interpolation for sink headers: secrets stay in the environment
:row.:describe: Sink header values resolve at send time: literal strings, {:env NAME} from the process environment, or {:command [pass show x]} from any local secret manager. effects.edn commits cleanly with zero secrets; failures name the header and source.

:row.:haystack: retitling: the title fact supersedes the created title; rollout names and renames children titles live in the immutable create event, so renaming is a superseding [:title] fact every lens prefers — history intact, display current. rollout titles children <process>: <repo> and re-runs retitle existing children idempotently. :feature names correct like facts; links read like a burndown 1eabd96 :green
:row.:title: retitling: the title fact supersedes the created title; rollout names and renames children
:row.:describe: Titles live in the immutable create event, so renaming is a superseding [:title] fact every lens prefers — history intact, display current. rollout titles children <process>: <repo> and re-runs retitle existing children idempotently.

:row.:haystack: h5 (phase 2): third parties accept attestations without trusting us witness countersigning plus ots anchoring produce attestations a third party accepts without trusting us the reproducible mode is never chosen over the attested mode — then the verifiability premium is imaginary and witnessed.dev is a plain saas mechanism leg live: head countersignatures as detached sidecars, verify l3 resolves witnesses to registered principals; economics/acceptance leg awaits third parties (h8) tik bundle (commit a051932)
:row.:title: H5 (Phase 2): third parties accept attestations without trusting us

:row.:haystack: authoring rules as edn: one source of truth feeding check and the llm prompt the naming smells become data: built-in rules are an edn vector, a store-level authoring-rules.edn adds org rules or disables built-ins by id, author check applies the merged set, and author prompt renders the same set plus the design philosophy — so an org that tightens its rules automatically re-teaches every llm its people copy the prompt into. share org-wide by putting the store in git. :feature lint and prompt are two lenses over one rule set; orgs extend it in their store eae6968 :green
:row.:title: authoring rules as EDN: one source of truth feeding check and the LLM prompt
:row.:describe: The naming smells become data: built-in rules are an EDN vector, a store-level authoring-rules.edn adds org rules or disables built-ins by id, author check applies the merged set, and author prompt renders the SAME set plus the design philosophy — so an org that tightens its rules automatically re-teaches every LLM its people copy the prompt into. Share org-wide by putting the store in git.

:row.:haystack: usability round: schema-aware set, honest rules label, ls --where, new-hint set coerces by the declared fact type: a bare a051932 stays a string when the process says string (explicit :colons still mean keywords) — zero quoting for declared facts, wired through set, probe and the email bridge. status names the pinned definition, not the create-time label, so migrated tickets stop lying. ls --where k=v filters the board by any fact. new prints the derived stage hint on stderr; usage shows whatif examples. :feature the schema ends the quoting wars; migrated tickets stop lying c356438 :green
:row.:title: usability round: schema-aware set, honest rules label, ls --where, new-hint
:row.:describe: set coerces by the DECLARED fact type: a bare a051932 stays a string when the process says string (explicit :colons still mean keywords) — zero quoting for declared facts, wired through set, probe and the email bridge. status names the PINNED definition, not the create-time label, so migrated tickets stop lying. ls --where k=v filters the board by any fact. new prints the derived stage hint on stderr; usage shows whatif examples.

:row.:haystack: lens memoization: content-keyed derived-state cache trigger measured and fired: 73x kernel, 4.3x warm board at 10k tickets :feature built: the content-keyed derived cache exactly as designed (event-filename fingerprint = input identity, validity horizon for time guards, attested-within never caches, disposable json per adr 0013) — plus the kernel fast path the benchmarks surfaced and bb bench / bb bench-store to keep performance claims measurable. a944024 :green

:row.:title: fuzz round 5: hostile DAGs, poisoned tickets, board fault isolation

:row.:haystack: fuzz layer round 2: argv, parsers, hostile-but-hash-valid store files new adversarial coverage for the surfaces built since round 1: random cli argv must exit 0/1 with words, never a stack trace; parse-value, similarity and author/check must be total over arbitrary strings and structures; usage-totals must fail well on hostile attestation bodies; and a store file whose name correctly hashes garbage bytes must be rejected cleanly at read time, not explode in the reader. :feature round 2 found the hash-valid-garbage hole; the reader now fails well a5eff10 :green
:row.:title: fuzz layer round 2: argv, parsers, hostile-but-hash-valid store files
:row.:describe: New adversarial coverage for the surfaces built since round 1: random CLI argv must exit 0/1 with words, never a stack trace; parse-value, similarity and author/check must be total over arbitrary strings and structures; usage-totals must fail well on hostile attestation bodies; and a store file whose NAME correctly hashes GARBAGE bytes must be rejected cleanly at read time, not explode in the reader.

:row.:haystack: store root + incremental verify (scale to millions) tik root: one canonical hash over sorted ticket->heads commits to the entire store (adr 0004 aggregated) — witness it and one countersignature timestamps everything; replicas compare roots in o(1). verify --changed skips tickets whose heads match the last full audit (disposable cache, adr 0013): drift detection, honestly weaker than full replay, which stays the audit :feature 0b32de8 :green
:row.:title: store root + incremental verify (scale to millions)
:row.:describe: tik root: one canonical hash over sorted ticket->heads commits to the entire store (ADR 0004 aggregated) — witness it and one countersignature timestamps everything; replicas compare roots in O(1). verify --changed skips tickets whose heads match the last full audit (disposable cache, ADR 0013): drift detection, honestly weaker than full replay, which stays the audit

:row.:haystack: tik probe: auto-derive facts from repo contents via per-process probe scripts a probe is any executable printing key=value lines; tik probe runs it per repo-carrying ticket with cwd set to that repo, asserts only changed values as ordinary signed facts, and reports which stages that derived. declared as :probe in the process definition or passed with --command; the kernel never learns probes exist. :feature the world answers for itself; probes record it, stages derive 8888398 :green
:row.:title: tik probe: auto-derive facts from repo contents via per-process probe scripts
:row.:describe: A probe is any executable printing key=value lines; tik probe runs it per repo-carrying ticket with cwd set to that repo, asserts only CHANGED values as ordinary signed facts, and reports which stages that derived. Declared as :probe in the process definition or passed with --command; the kernel never learns probes exist.

:row.:haystack: hidden .tik store and git-style upward root discovery the store can live in a .tik directory and every command discovers its root git-style: tik_root, else the nearest ancestor with .tik, else the nearest ancestor with tickets/, else the cwd. tik init [--hidden] creates the skeleton and says so. a portfolio store above many repos becomes one dot-entry, reachable from anywhere beneath it. :feature one dot-entry above many repos; every command finds it from anywhere beneath 9f6ab65 :green
:row.:title: hidden .tik store and git-style upward root discovery
:row.:describe: The store can live in a .tik directory and every command discovers its root git-style: TIK_ROOT, else the nearest ancestor with .tik, else the nearest ancestor with tickets/, else the cwd. tik init [--hidden] creates the skeleton and says so. A portfolio store above many repos becomes one dot-entry, reachable from anywhere beneath it.

:row.:haystack: status: pinned rules line misread as ticket identity process: @ sha256 read like the ticket id could change; relabeled rules: vn (pinned @ hash) — ticket uuids are immutable, the hash names the rule set :bug 5995a14 :green
:row.:title: status: pinned rules line misread as ticket identity
:row.:describe: process: @ sha256 read like the ticket id could change; relabeled rules: vN (pinned @ hash) — ticket UUIDs are immutable, the hash names the rule set

:row.:haystack: link section ux: status-first, process-ordered, deduplicated, out of the facts table link and title-override facts leave the rendered facts table (their sections own the display). link lines lead with the derived stage, then id and title, the rel only when the title does not already say it; ordering follows stage depth in the target process — ancestor count, so parallel branches tie and fall back alphabetical. :feature names correct like facts; links read like a burndown 87326e3 :green
:row.:title: link section UX: status-first, process-ordered, deduplicated, out of the facts table
:row.:describe: Link and title-override facts leave the rendered facts table (their sections own the display). Link lines lead with the derived stage, then id and title, the rel only when the title does not already say it; ordering follows stage depth in the TARGET process — ancestor count, so parallel branches tie and fall back alphabetical.

:row.:haystack: anti-staleness: live link facts and prose-rot heuristics link.<rel>=<ticket-id> facts render the target ticket with its current derived stage in status and ls --long, so cross-ticket references never rot. the store lint flags the two mechanical tells of prose staleness: a description older than the latest landing on its own ticket, and status-report phrasing or ticket ids inside descriptions (use a link fact instead). :feature references derive, prose wears its age ae60136 :green
:row.:title: anti-staleness: live link facts and prose-rot heuristics
:row.:describe: link.<rel>=<ticket-id> facts render the target ticket with its CURRENT derived stage in status and ls --long, so cross-ticket references never rot. The store lint flags the two mechanical tells of prose staleness: a description older than the latest landing on its own ticket, and status-report phrasing or ticket ids inside descriptions (use a link fact instead).

:row.:title: color: lovable CLI output
:row.:describe: stage-aware colors in ls/next/status, green checks red crosses in explain — tty-detected, NO_COLOR honored, kernel lens stays plain data

:row.:haystack: fuzz round 9: the oidc bridge (hostile idp responses and jwts) :feature make the oidc bridge total over a hostile identity provider: jwt decode and all idp-response parsing fail well with ex-info naming the idp, never a raw npe/parse crash reworded as a tik bug a broken or malicious idp can no longer crash the bridge with a raw exception — every parse boundary fails well and names the idp 055619a :green

:row.:haystack: sink armory: nine more notification adapters plus the universal command sink ntfy, gotify, pushover, telegram, teams, mattermost, rocketchat, googlechat and opsgenie join slack/discord/matrix/alertmanager/pagerduty/webhook/email; sinks may set auth :headers; and a :command sink pipes the json payload to any program — notify-send, sms gateways, syslog, whatever the shop already has. :feature seventeen sink types, templated, plus a universal command escape hatch d033fd0 :green
:row.:title: sink armory: nine more notification adapters plus the universal command sink
:row.:describe: ntfy, gotify, pushover, telegram, teams, mattermost, rocketchat, googlechat and opsgenie join slack/discord/matrix/alertmanager/pagerduty/webhook/email; sinks may set auth :headers; and a :command sink pipes the JSON payload to ANY program — notify-send, SMS gateways, syslog, whatever the shop already has.

:row.:haystack: meta-check: every public kernel fn must carry a totality verdict (registered or exempt) :feature sweep the pure kernel namespaces and fail if any public fn is neither in the totality-registry nor in an explicit exemptions map, so a new boundary cannot silently skip its fail-well verdict a new public kernel fn now fails the suite until it is classified — total-boundary or exempt-with-reason 29b6877 :green
:row.:title: meta-check: every public kernel fn must carry a totality verdict (registered or exempt)
:row.:describe: sweep the pure kernel namespaces and fail if any public fn is neither in the totality-registry nor in an explicit exemptions map, so a new boundary cannot silently skip its fail-well verdict

:row.:haystack: explain: fact= sugar double-reports an absent fact [:fact= p v] expands to [:and [:fact p] [:malli ...]]; when p is absent both conjuncts fail and explain shows fact/missing plus a redundant schema missing-required-key line. design question: smarter expansion, reason dedup in the lens, or revisit the v6 demotion of fact= (it had a clean single mismatch reason). found by h1 dogfood on the h3 ticket :bug dc39ea4 :green
:row.:title: explain: fact= sugar double-reports an absent fact
:row.:describe: [:fact= p v] expands to [:and [:fact p] [:malli ...]]; when p is absent both conjuncts fail and explain shows fact/missing PLUS a redundant schema missing-required-key line. Design question: smarter expansion, reason dedup in the lens, or revisit the v6 demotion of fact= (it had a clean single mismatch reason). Found by H1 dogfood on the H3 ticket
